Class BiasSweepWorker

java.lang.Object
javax.swing.SwingWorker<Boolean, lcr.business.BiasDataPoint>
lcr.business.BiasSweepWorker
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable, Future<Boolean>, RunnableFuture<Boolean>
public class BiasSweepWorker extends SwingWorker<Boolean, lcr.business.BiasDataPoint>
Asynchronous worker that performs a bias voltage sweep (0-499) on a background thread and updates the UI chart in real time. * @author Sylkat

  • Nested Class Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    protected Boolean
    doInBackground()
    Executes the hardware sweep loop in the background.
    protected void
    done()
    Handles post-execution cleanup and resets hardware bias back to zero.
    protected void
    process(List<lcr.business.BiasDataPoint> chunks)
    Updates the UI chart using published data points on the Event Dispatch Thread.

    • BiasSweepWorker

      public BiasSweepWorker(SerialConnection serialPort, RealTimeChartPanel chartPanel, BiasSweepDialog biasSweepDialog)
      Initializes the worker with required hardware and UI references. * @param serialPort the active serial connection handler
      Parameters:
      chartPanel - the main chart panel reference
      biasSweepDialog - the dialog displaying the sweep progress

  • Method Details

    • doInBackground

      protected Boolean doInBackground() throws Exception
      Executes the hardware sweep loop in the background. * @return true if the execution finishes completely
      Specified by:
      doInBackground in class SwingWorker<Boolean, lcr.business.BiasDataPoint>
      Throws:
      Exception - if an error occurs during the serial communication

    • process

      protected void process(List<lcr.business.BiasDataPoint> chunks)
      Updates the UI chart using published data points on the Event Dispatch Thread. * @param chunks the list of data points accumulated to update
      Overrides:
      process in class SwingWorker<Boolean, lcr.business.BiasDataPoint>

    • done

      protected void done()
      Handles post-execution cleanup and resets hardware bias back to zero.
      Overrides:
      done in class SwingWorker<Boolean, lcr.business.BiasDataPoint>
